ddls:
CREATE TABLE "emails" (
  "email_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each email */,
  "email_text" TEXT /* Full text content of the email */,
  "sender_email" TEXT /* Email address of the sender */,
  "subject" TEXT /* Subject of the email */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Date and time the email was received */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the email category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "unigrams" TEXT /* List of unigrams extracted from the email text */,
  "binary_category" TEXT /* Binary representation of the email's category */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who manually categorized the email */,
  "is_manual" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the categorization was done manually */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the email was sent */,
  "spam_score" REAL /* Score indicating the likelihood of the email being spam */,
  "read_status" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the email has been read */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("email_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_emails_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_emails_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parent category, if applicable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "unigrams" (
  "unigram_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unigram entry */,
  "email_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the email associated with the unigram */,
  "unigram" TEXT /* The unigram extracted from the email text */,
  "frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency of the unigram in the email */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unigram_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_unigrams_email_id FOREIGN KEY ("email_id") REFERENCES emails ("email_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "binary_categories" (
  "binary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each binary representation entry */,
  "email_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the email associated with the binary representation */,
  "binary_representation" TEXT /* Binary array representation of category for the email */,
  "model_version" TEXT /* Version of the machine learning model used to generate the binary representation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("binary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_binary_categories_email_id FOREIGN KEY ("email_id") REFERENCES emails ("email_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_read_status" (
  "read_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each read status entry */,
  "email_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the email */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who read the email */,
  "read_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the email was read */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("read_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_read_status_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_read_status_email_id FOREIGN KEY ("email_id") REFERENCES emails ("email_id")
)
